title: Allergic reaction

items.final_wording: Recognises rapid symptoms with airway, breathing, or circulation problems and usually skin or mucosal changes such as urticaria or angioedema; skin changes alone are not anaphylaxis.

source.title: Emergency treatment of anaphylaxis: Guidelines for healthcare providers
source.evidence_paraphrase: Recognises rapid symptoms with airway, breathing, or circulation problems and usually skin or mucosal changes such as urticaria or angioedema; skin changes alone are not anaphylaxis.

items.rationale: Supports mapped rapid airway, breathing, circulation, mucosal, skin, syncope, hypotension, wheeze, stridor, vital, respiratory, and airway fields.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting known allergen exposure, food or drug triggers, action-plan discussion, and specialist follow-up after suspected anaphylaxis.

source.title: Emergency treatment of anaphylaxis: Guidelines for healthcare providers
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting known allergen exposure, food or drug triggers, action-plan discussion, and specialist follow-up after suspected anaphylaxis.

items.rationale: Supports mapped trigger, allergy history, action or safety discussion, return, escalation, and specialist follow-up fields.

items.final_wording: Defines urticaria as a mast-cell-driven disease presenting with wheals, angioedema, or both and distinguishes acute and chronic forms.

source.title: The international EAACI/GA²LEN/EuroGuiDerm/APAAACI guideline for the definition, classification, diagnosis, and management of urticaria
source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines urticaria as a mast-cell-driven disease presenting with wheals, angioedema, or both and distinguishes acute and chronic forms.

items.rationale: Supports mapped wheal or urticaria, itching, and angioedema fields without equating skin findings with anaphylaxis.

items.final_wording: States that chronic spontaneous or inducible urticaria can impair quality of life and work or school performance and that the guideline covers classification, diagnosis, and management.

source.title: The international EAACI/GA²LEN/EuroGuiDerm/APAAACI guideline for the definition, classification, diagnosis, and management of urticaria
source.evidence_paraphrase: States that chronic spontaneous or inducible urticaria can impair quality of life and work or school performance and that the guideline covers classification, diagnosis, and management.

items.rationale: Supports the urticaria scope reviewed; no additional treatment item is mapped.
